statement.en: Replay-buffer engineering for noise-robust quantum circuit optimization
statement.summary: Deep reinforcement learning (RL) for quantum circuit optimization faces three fundamental bottlenecks: replay buffers that ignore the reliability of temporal-difference (TD) targets, curriculum-based architecture search that triggers a full quantum-classical evaluation at every environment step, and the routine discard of noiseless trajectories when retraining under hardware noise. We address all three by treating the replay buffer as a primary algorithmic lever for quantum optimization. We introduce ReaPER$+$, an annealed replay rule that transitions from TD error-driven prioritization early in training to reliability-aware sampling as value estimates mature, achieving $4-32\times$ gains in sample efficiency over fixed PER, ReaPER, and uniform replay while consistently discovering more co

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a preprint (dated 2026-04-25, future-dated, likely preprocessing artifact or submission deadline) proposing ReaPER+, a hybrid replay-buffer strategy for quantum circuit optimization. The core claims are empirical (4-32× sample efficiency gains) and contingent on: (1) successful validation across multiple quantum hardware backends, (2) generalization beyond the tested circuit classes, and (3) reproducibility of reported benchmarks. The abstract truncates mid-sentence, preventing full assessment. The method combines established RL techniques (PER, annealing schedules) with quantum-specific noise modeling—theoretically sound but experimentally unvalidated in this snapshot. Classification as FLOWING reflects: preprint status, incomplete abstract, and dependence on experimental replication.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: ノイズの中で、誤差の鋭さと値推定の信頼が踊るように優先度を決める秘密の式。
